runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_dedup_policy: Deduplicate nodes by semantic identity. Do not assume any organization-specific naming convention. Keep one canonical node when items represent the same physical bin/buffer/location, even if IDs differ. Prefer IDs explicitly present on maps/tables; otherwise normalize ID from label. Merge metadata fields into the canonical node.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_exclusion_policy: Exclude canceled/inactive/decommissioned/removed/closed trash bins or buffer points. Do not output such items as nodes even if present in source artifacts.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_joint_prompt: Extract all bin/buffer node metadata by jointly analyzing the map image and support artifact image. Be exhaustive and preserve IDs exactly as they appear (e.g., L.1, TR.12).
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_fallback_prompt: Extract all bin/buffer node metadata from the provided support artifact image. Be exhaustive and preserve IDs exactly as they appear (e.g., L.1, TR.12).
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_delta_prompt: Extract any additional bin metadata missed previously from the support artifact image.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_normalize_prompt: Normalize the provided extracted content into JSON nodes.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_normalize_context: Produce JSON only with shape {"nodes": [...]} where each node includes id/label/type/metadata when available. Keep IDs exactly as seen in source text (e.g., L.1, TR.12).
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_delta_context: Return concise text only. Focus on IDs and attributes not already extracted.
runtime_prompts.compact_output_policy: Minimize output tokens: return only required fields, no explanations, no duplicated rows, no markdown wrappers unless explicitly requested.
runtime_prompts.tabular_extraction_output_hint: Return CSV plain text only, with a header row.
runtime_prompts.tabular_extraction_no_support_hint: No support table image was uploaded; extract any visible tabular metadata from the map image.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_symbol_output_hint: Return a concise markdown table of symbols and notation. Process only the provided single map image.
runtime_prompts.edge_extraction_dedup_policy: Deduplicate edges by topology. If multiple rows describe the same traversal link, keep one canonical edge with the best available cost/label. Avoid source-target duplicates regardless of ID formatting.
runtime_prompts.edge_extraction_csv_fallback_hint: If JSON cannot be produced, return CSV with header: Source Node,Target Node,Approximate Cost

extractmap_symbol.prompt: Analyze the provided map image and extract all map symbols along with their corresponding text notations. Output the results as a Markdown table with two columns: 'Symbol Description' and 'Notation'.

extractmap_text.task_description: Extract node metadata and update existing JSON.
extractmap_text.note: Process only one image at a time to ensure high extraction accuracy.
extractmap_text.prompt: Analyze the provided map image and extract all text and symbols located near the 'buffer' labels. Cross-reference this with the provided buffer trait table. Update the provided incomplete JSON file with this new data. Categorize each item with a node type label (e.g., 'building', 'indoor_buffer', 'outdoor_buffer'). Output the final, completed data strictly in JSON format.

tabular_extraction.task_description: Convert visual tables into structured data.
tabular_extraction.prompt: Extract all data from the provided table image. Output the extracted data in CSV format, ensuring all column headers and row alignments perfectly match the visual table.

edge_extraction.task_description: Build a traversal graph with estimated edge costs.
edge_extraction.prompt: Using the provided map image and the reference node data from the JSON file, identify all possible traversal paths between nodes. Estimate the traversal cost (distance) based on visual scale. Create a comprehensive list of edges for a traversal graph. Export this data as a CSV file containing exactly three columns: 'Source Node', 'Target Node', and 'Approximate Cost'.
